output: Implemented batch 4 / T4 by applying touch-aware selection and move rules, adding visible clip and shot-group action triggers for phone/tablet layouts, and wiring precision mode into keyboard seeking without changing desktop or hardware-keyboard defaults. Touch `Select` mode now toggles clip membership, touch `Move` mode preserves an additive selection unless a drag actually starts, and non-hover touch entry points now expose clip and shot-group actions that previously depended on context-menu or hover-only affordances. Verified with type-checking plus the full `ClipAction`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `useClipDrag` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_4.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Applied the selection, move, context, shot-group, and precision contract through the existing provider-backed editor stack. Added touch-selection helpers to the shared interaction model, taught `useClipDrag` to treat touch `Select` taps as toggle selection and touch `Move` taps as selection-preserving when a drag never starts, and extended `useMultiSelect` / `useTimelineSelection` with a no-collapse preserve path so additive selections survive touch move attempts. Added visible overflow affordances for clip and shot-group actions on phone/tablet layouts so navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching are reachable without hover or `onContextMenu`-only entry points. Added precision-aware keyboard seeking with frame-step `Alt` + arrow behavior gated by precision mode, while leaving desktop and hardware-keyboard defaults unchanged otherwise. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Phone and iPad touch selection and move behavior now follow the shared interaction contract instead of desktop-only modifier assumptions: touch `Select` mode toggles clip membership, touch `Move` mode preserves additive selections unless a drag actually begins, and desktop plus hardware-keyboard semantics remain on the existing path because the new branching only activates for touch modality. Clip and shot-group actions that were previously hover- or context-menu-gated now have visible touch triggers, and precision mode adds frame-step arrow-key seeking without replacing the existing desktop shortcut behavior.
